title: angelica pubescens f. glabra
intro: Angelica pubescens f. glabra is a subspecies of the Angelica pubescens plant, a perennial herb native to mountainous regions of East Asia, characterized by its glabrous (hairless) stems and leaves. The plant is used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Chinese medicine, where it is valued for its anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antispasmodic effects. The health benefits of Angelica pubescens f. glabra include reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often combined with other herbs to enhance their therapeutic effects.
